name: Potential Indicator Removal Activity on Host
notes: This rule triggers when an adversary delete or modify artifacts generated on a host system to remove evidence of their presence or hinder defenses.
bb_references: BB:BehaviorDefinition: Terminal Server Client Connection History Cleared - Registry
conditions_text: when an event matches any of the following BB:BehaviorDefinition: Fsutil Suspicious Invocation, BB:BehaviorDefinition: Possible Unauthorized MBR Modifications, BB:BehaviorDefinition: Suspicious Load of Advapi31, BB:BehaviorDefinition: Disable of ETW Trace, BB:BehaviorDefinition: Sysmon Driver Unload, BB:BehaviorDefinition: Clearing Windows Console History, BB:BehaviorDefinition: Terminal Server Client Connection History Cleared - Registry
TD_list: APPLY Potential Indicator Removal Activity on Host on events which are detected by the LOCAL system

AND when an event matches any of the following BB:BehaviorDefinition: Fsutil Suspicious Invocation, BB:BehaviorDefinition: Possible Unauthorized MBR Modifications, BB:BehaviorDefinition: Suspicious Load of Advapi31, BB:BehaviorDefinition: Disable of ETW Trace, BB:BehaviorDefinition: Sysmon Driver Unload, BB:BehaviorDefinition: Clearing Windows Console History, BB:BehaviorDefinition: Terminal Server Client Connection History Cleared - Registry
RED_list: Event name: Potential Indicator Removal Activity on Host
Event description: Detects when an adversary delete or modify artifacts generated on a host system to remove evidence of their presence or hinder defenses.
Force the dispatched event to create a NEW offense, select the offense using Machine Identifier (custom)
This information should contribute to the name of the associated offense(s)
